## Appearances & Description

Mark owns a little black-and-white [monkey](wiki:Monkey)(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Racok22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) from the [Summer Islands](wiki:Summer_Islands).(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Raffc14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

## Narrative Arc

### A Clash of Kings

Ser Mark is amongst the nobles who join [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) beneath the walls of [Bitterbridge](wiki:Bitterbridge). He feeds his [monkey](wiki:Monkey) morsels at the feast.(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Racok22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth) later recalls that Mark was a part of the wager over who would claim her [maidenhead](wiki:Maidenhead). He used his monkey to make her laugh in his attempt to woo her, but Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly) found out about the wager and put an end to it.(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Raffc14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1) After the bet was discovered and ended, Brienne sought out and defeated Mark during the [melee at Bitterbridge](wiki:Melee_at_Bitterbridge).(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Raffc20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

Mark is among those who go over to [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) after the death of Renly at [Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)). Mark fights at the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater), losing part of his left arm and his monkey before getting captured by forces loyal to King [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon). He bends the knee when presented to [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

### A Storm of Swords

Mark is a [sworn sword](wiki:Sworn_sword) of Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell).(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Rasosappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

### A Feast for Crows

Mark is rumored to be [Megga Tyrell](wiki:Megga_Tyrell)'s choice for a husband by [Taena of Myr](wiki:Taena_of_Myr), as Megga has asked her cousin [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell) for help in finding Mark a new monkey.(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Raffc28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) has an arrest warrant signed for Mark as part of her plans to remove Margaery. He is sent to the dungeons of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) after his arrest.(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Raffc43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)

### A Dance with Dragons

Mark is held with the rest of Margaery's falsely-accused lovers in a dungeon under the charge of [Qyburn](wiki:Qyburn).(wiki:Mark_Mullendore.cite_ref-Radwdappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

Matthew participated in the [tourney at Ashford Meadow](wiki:Tourney_at_Ashford_Meadow) in [209 AC](wiki:209_AC).(wiki:Matthew_Mullendore.cite_ref-Rthk_.5B.5Bthe_hedge_knight_.28comics.29.7Cgraphic_novel.5D.5D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

1	## Origins
2	
3	Under Jack's watch, [King-Beyond-the-Wall](wiki:King-Beyond-the-Wall) [Raymun Redbeard](wiki:Raymun_Redbeard) and his [free folk](wiki:Free_folk) host slipped past the [Wall](wiki:Wall) and headed south. This drew the forces of [House Umber](wiki:House_Umber) and [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark) to [Long Lake](wiki:Long_Lake), where Raymun and Lord [Willam Stark](wiki:Willam_Stark) were [killed in battle](wiki:Battle_at_Long_Lake). The [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) arrived too late to participate in the battle and were ordered by [Artos Stark](wiki:Artos_Stark) to dispose of the dead. This series of events led Sleepy Jack to earn his nickname.(wiki:Jack_Musgood.cite_ref-Radwd7.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Jack_Musgood.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_wall_and_beyond:_the_wildlings.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

## Origins

Under Jack's watch, [King-Beyond-the-Wall](wiki:King-Beyond-the-Wall) [Raymun Redbeard](wiki:Raymun_Redbeard) and his [free folk](wiki:Free_folk) host slipped past the [Wall](wiki:Wall) and headed south. This drew the forces of [House Umber](wiki:House_Umber) and [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark) to [Long Lake](wiki:Long_Lake), where Raymun and Lord [Willam Stark](wiki:Willam_Stark) were [killed in battle](wiki:Battle_at_Long_Lake). The [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) arrived too late to participate in the battle and were ordered by [Artos Stark](wiki:Artos_Stark) to dispose of the dead. This series of events led Sleepy Jack to earn his nickname.(wiki:Jack_Musgood.cite_ref-Radwd7.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Jack_Musgood.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_wall_and_beyond:_the_wildlings.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

file.content:
## Narrative Arc

### A Feast for Crows

Pinchface Jon brings a dozen longships in support of [Euron Greyjoy](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy) after he declares himself [King of the Isles and the North](wiki:King_of_the_Isles_and_the_North).(wiki:Jon_Myre.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) He supports Euron at the [kingsmoot on Old Wyk](wiki:Kingsmoot_on_Old_Wyk), where he trades insults with [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy).(wiki:Jon_Myre.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

### The Winds of Winter

Jon feasts in the ruins of a castle near the [Arbor](wiki:Arbor) with some of Euron's men, and he takes part in mocking [Aeron Greyjoy](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy) when the latter is brought up from the hold of the *[Silence](wiki:Silence)*.(wiki:Jon_Myre.cite_ref-4)

## Quotes

> **Jon**: This girl forgets herself. [Balon](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy) let her believe she was a man. **Asha**: Your father made the same mistake with you.(wiki:Jon_Myre.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
>
> —Jon and [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy)

Good! Now I have all the prose. Let me process each file and create the output JSONLs. I'll work through them efficiently:

**matthew-mullendore (2 candidates):**
1. matthew-mullendore → 209-ac: "participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow in 209 AC" → 209 AC is just a date/year, not an entity to have an edge to. **reject** as `temporal-reference-not-edge`

2. matthew-mullendore → tourney-at-ashford-meadow: "Matthew participated in the tourney at Ashford Meadow" → **emit_edge** FIGHTS_IN tourney-at-ashford-meadow. Tier-1.

**jack-musgood (10 candidates):**
1. jack-musgood → artos-stark: "ordered by Artos Stark to dispose of the dead" → Artos commands Jack. From Jack's perspective: TAKES_ORDERS_FROM? or SERVES? Actually looking at the manual, there's COMMANDS which is "leader → follower". The reverse would be something like OBEYS or SERVES. From Jack's perspective: SERVES artos-stark. Tier-2 (inferred from "ordered by"). Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. "ordered by" = commanded. The commander → commanded relationship. So this would be from Artos's node as COMMANDS. From Jack's node, it's the reverse. Looking at the manual, SERVES seems right. Actually, let me check if there's a specific "follows order of" edge. Looking at the vocab, I see COMMANDS (line 245) but no explicit reverse in the list. So from Jack's perspective, I should check if this is a reverse-direction edge that belongs on Artos's node. Actually, Commands is typically a standing relationship (military authority), while "ordered" might be a one-time action. Let me emit SERVES since Jack is doing what Artos commands. 

The directionality check shows SERVES goes server → liege, so Jack SERVES Artos fits the canonical direction. But I need to consider whether this is truly a service relationship or more of a military chain of command—Jack is a Night's Watch member and Artos is a historical commander of the Watch, making this organizational rather than personal service. Let me look for SWORN_TO or MEMBER_OF as better fits for this institutional relationship.

The context "ordered by Artos Stark" in a specific battle suggests military command rather than standing service. I don't have OBEYS in the vocabulary, and COMMANDS belongs on the commander's side, not Jack's. Since the action logically belongs with Artos as the one issuing orders, I should reject this edge as `command-action-belongs-on-commander-node`.

Wait—the manual explicitly covers SERVES at lines 245 and 288, stating never to emit SERVED_BY on the liege's node. From Jack's perspective following Artos's command, SERVES fits as the appropriate edge. This is tier-2 inferred, so I'll emit SERVES pointing to artos-stark.

For Jack and the battle-at-long-lake: the candidate proposes an edge, but the text explicitly states Jack "arrived too late to participate." He was not a participant in this battle, only aware of it. This should be rejected as `observer-of-event-not-participant`.

The free folk mention presents a similar issue—they "slipped past" under Jack's watch, but the prose frames this as background context for the invasion rather than a direct relational edge. Jack didn't fight them or have an explicit relationship; he failed to prevent them, which isn't a valid edge type. Rejecting as `mentioned-context-for-invasion-not-relational`.

For House Stark, the forces drew to Long Lake but Jack has no direct connection to the house in the text—he's simply mentioned in proximity to the event.

House Umber's forces similarly appear without establishing a Jack-to-Umber relationship.

The King-Beyond-the-Wall is more interesting: Jack's role as ranger meant he should have stopped Raymun Redbeard from crossing, suggesting potential opposition or surveillance. However, the prose doesn't explicitly state this relationship—it's narrative context about Jack's failure to prevent the crossing. This might constitute a confrontation given Jack's defensive position at the Wall, but the text doesn't make the relationship explicit enough.

Long Lake is simply a battle location where Jack wasn't present. Raymun Redbeard himself presents a similar issue: the passage establishes that Jack failed to stop him, but doesn't describe actual engagement or opposition. The Wall is Jack's post, not a relational target.

Willam Stark's death occurred in battle without Jack's involvement—he arrived afterward, so no edge applies. Most of Jack's candidates collapse into narrative context rather than meaningful graph relationships. The artos-stark SERVES connection remains the strongest candidate.

For jon-myre, trading insults with Asha Greyjoy suggests OPPOSES, which exists in the vocabulary. This warrants an edge emission at Tier-2 confidence based on the inferred antagonism, though I need to check if there's additional context in the second mention. location near the Arbor where he feasts, so I'll mark this as TRAVELS_TO. The relationship is inferred from the feasting activity rather than explicit travel mention.

For the Silence, Jon's connection is ambiguous—he's present at the scene where Aeron is brought from the ship's hold, but there's no clear evidence he's crew or aboard. I should reject this without stronger indication of his actual role on the vessel.

The second mention of Asha involves dialogue between them, which suggests opposition or conflict, so I'll emit that edge.

Balon appears only as a reference Asha makes in conversation, not as a direct interaction with Jon, so that's worth rejecting as an indirect mention rather than a substantive relationship.
